Petition For Approval Of Agreement Settling Will Contest, Including Admission Of Will To Proabte And Appointment Of Administrator
Off Calendar Mar-02-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Russell Farmer (Petitioner/Respondent). Kai Wessels for and with Joe Martori, for Mary Martori and for Catherine Villegas. Michael Wooten also present.

The Court stated that it had the opportunity to review the settlement agreement, questioned the amounts of the preliminary distributions and questioned why Mr. Wooten was being appointed when Debra Dolch was already in place. Mr. Wessels explained that Mr. Wooten was appointed because the Martori's requested him. Mr. Wooten also explained that Andrew DeVries would be assisting with sale of the properties. Mr. Koplowitz wants to make sure that Mr. Wooten is aware and prepared to sell the properties as agreed in the settlement agreement, and expressed concern about priority of payment to his Client, his Cleint being paid at the earliest possible date and about the matter dragging on. The Court stated that a Petition for Preliminary Distribution was needed pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement, and expressed concern about potential conflict with Mr. Wessels representing Mr. Wooten and fees. Status on filing of Ms. Dolch's final accounting was set for April 3, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the settlement agreement is granted, the Petition for Probate filed by Mr. Koplowitz will go off calendar and the will contest will be dismissed. The Petition for Approval of Agreement Settling Will Contest, etc., as well as the two probate petitions were taken under submission for the Court to review the language of the order. Mr. Wessels agreed to withdraw representation of Mr. Wooten to avoid any conflict issues. Mr. Wooten will select other Counsel to represent him and will submit an order for probate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Weatherly.

Off Calendar Jul-05-2011 Continued To Aug-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Russell Farmer.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that the mediation would be held next week with Carolyn Hinshaw, that the parties have started some discovery, and that Ms. Dolch will continue as Special Administrator for now. The Court continued the matter to August 2, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to advise the Examiner in advance of the hearing if the matter settles.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-10-2011 Continued To Jul-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Russell Farmer with respect to the will contest. Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (Special Administrator). Kai Wessels for the Martori (will Contestants).

Mr. Koplowitz stated that he filed his objection to the competing petition and a formal answer to contest, that they are just starting settlement discussions and that they agree that this is an appropriate case for Pro Bono Mediation. The Parties agreed to the appointment of Debra Dolch as Special Administrator with general powers with limited authority under Independent Administration of the Estates Act until further order of the Court. Mr. Der is to submit the order and file an ex parte petition to increase bond if needed. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation (order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation signed in open Court). Mediation is to be completed by June 10, 2011. The Parties are to report to the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by June 30, 2011. Both petitions were continued to July 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sean Miller for Russell Farmer. Kai Wessels for Mary Martori, Joe Martori and possibly one other Beneficiary.

Mr. Wessels requested a continuance to properly object to the appointment of Russell Farmer, to the will being admitted and stated that he anticipates this being a will contest. Mr. Miller expressed concern about rents, insurance, etc. Counsel held discussions outside of the courtroom. Counsel did not stipulate to appointment of a Special Administrator. The matter was continued to April 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
